extra.reasoning:
The property is VIOLATED. leg_swelling appears in exactly one rule: [interaction_drop, priority 100] IF (palpitations AND vision_loss AND abdominal_pain AND pallor AND stiff_neck AND leg_swelling) THEN acuity := self_care. This rule has the highest priority (100) and assigns the LOWEST acuity (self_care). leg_swelling is a required conjunct.

Consider the baseline input with palpitations=true, vision_loss=true, abdominal_pain=true, pallor=true, stiff_neck=true, sweating=false, back_pain=false, and leg_swelling=FALSE. The interaction_drop rule does not fire (leg_swelling missing). Firing rules include raise_pallor (priority 3 -> emergent) and raise_vision_loss (priority 3 -> emergent), plus several priority-2 urgent rules. The highest-priority matching rule is priority 3, so acuity = emergent (the highest level).

Now set leg_swelling=TRUE, leaving every other input unchanged (the witness assignment given). Now all six conjuncts of interaction_drop are satisfied, so it fires at priority 100 -- higher than the priority-3 emergent rules. It assigns acuity := self_care. The highest-priority matching rule is now interaction_drop, so acuity = self_care (the lowest level).

Setting leg_swelling true thus drops acuity from emergent down to self_care, directly violating the monotonicity property that setting leg_swelling true must never lower acuity. The witness_assignment is the post-flip input (leg_swelling=true) that produces self_care while its leg_swelling=false counterpart produces emergent.
